text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. A key principle is responsive design, meaning the app must adapt smoothly to different screen sizes and orientations. Developers often choose between native or cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific system, like iOS or Android, while cross-platform apps work on multiple systems using one codebase. After development, the app is submitted to app stores, such as Google Play or the Apple App Store, where users can download it. Performance is crucial—a well-optimized app runs quickly, uses less battery, and provides a better user experience.
